seoTitle: How to Use Don't Summarize in Power BI Tables
metaDescription: Show raw, unaggregated values in Power BI tables using the Don't Summarize setting, useful when totals or averages hide the detail you need.
intro: Power BI aggregates numeric fields by default, which is helpful for totals but can hide the exact raw values you sometimes need in a table. This guide shows how to use the Don't Summarize setting to display data exactly as it appears in your source, without automatic summing or averaging. It's a small setting that can prevent confusion in detailed reports.

steps.heading: Find the Don't Summarize setting
steps.body: Locate the field's aggregation options in your table or matrix visual, where Power BI lets you choose how a numeric column is calculated.

steps.heading: Switch the field to Don't Summarize
steps.body: Change the aggregation setting from sum, average, or another calculation to Don't Summarize so the raw value is shown as-is.

steps.heading: Check the table's display
steps.body: Review the table to confirm it's now showing individual raw values instead of an aggregated total.

steps.heading: Understand when raw values matter most
steps.body: Use this setting in situations where precise, unaggregated figures are important, such as auditing records or reviewing individual transactions.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for accurate reporting
steps.body: Apply Don't Summarize selectively, since showing raw values everywhere can make large tables harder to read than a properly aggregated view.

faq.question: Why does Power BI automatically summarize my numbers?
faq.answer: By default, Power BI treats numeric fields as measures that need aggregation, such as sum or average, unless you tell it otherwise.

faq.question: Does Don't Summarize work on any numeric field?
faq.answer: Yes, it's an aggregation option available for numeric columns in table and matrix visuals.

faq.question: Will using Don't Summarize slow down my report?
faq.answer: Not typically, since it changes how a value is displayed rather than adding significant processing overhead.

faq.question: Can I switch a field back to summarized after using Don't Summarize?
faq.answer: Yes, the aggregation setting can be changed back to sum, average, or another option at any time.

faq.question: When should I avoid using Don't Summarize?
faq.answer: Avoid it in large tables where showing every raw value would overwhelm viewers who need a summarized overview instead.
